articles.title: Now Meta will track what employees do on their computers to train its AI agents
articles.summary: Meta is installing tracking software on US employees' computers to record mouse movements, keystrokes, and screenshots for training its AI agents to automate work tasks.

articles.title: Anthropic’s most dangerous AI model just fell into the wrong hands
articles.summary: Anthropic's Mythos AI model, designed for cybersecurity, was accessed by unauthorized users through a third-party contractor's credentials and internet tools. The powerful model posed security risks if misused.

articles.title: SpaceX cuts a deal to maybe buy Cursor for $60 billion
articles.summary: SpaceX plans to acquire AI coding platform Cursor for $60 billion or pay a $10 billion fee, aiming to strengthen xAI's competitive position against Anthropic in the AI market.

articles.title: OpenAI teams up with Infosys to bring AI tools to more businesses
articles.summary: OpenAI partners with Infosys to deliver AI tools to businesses. The collaboration focuses on modernizing software development, automating workflows, and deploying AI systems across engineering and DevOps.

articles.title: AI is spitting out more potential drugs than ever. This startup wants to figure out which ones matter.
articles.summary: 10x Science raised $4.8 million to help pharmaceutical researchers understand complex molecules generated by AI, enabling them to identify the most promising drug candidates.

articles.title: Google Maps is about to get a big dose of AI
articles.summary: Google Maps is integrating generative AI to enhance its features, making the mapping service more intelligent and capable.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The platform has attracted 2 million developers by addressing limitations of legacy cloud infrastructure for AI applications.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI coding, but Goose offers identical free open-source functionality that runs locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The unconventional recruitment stunt successfully attracted thousands of talented engineers to the company.

articles.title: Contrary to popular superstition, AES 128 is just fine in a post-quantum world
articles.summary: Cryptography engineer Filippo Valsorda clarifies that AES-128 encryption remains secure against quantum computing threats, contrary to popular misconception. The 128-bit standard offers optimal balance between security and computational efficiency.

articles.title: US-sanctioned currency exchange says $15 million heist done by "unfriendly states"
articles.summary: US-sanctioned cryptocurrency exchange Grinex halted operations after a $15 million heist, blaming state-sponsored hackers from "unfriendly states." The Kyrgyzstan-registered exchange claimed the attack targeted Russian users and damaged the nation's financial sovereignty.

articles.title: Recent advances push Big Tech closer to the Q-Day danger zone
articles.summary: Recent cryptographic advances bring quantum computing threats closer to reality. The 2010 Flame malware exploited MD5 weaknesses to forge digital certificates, demonstrating risks that quantum computers could amplify globally.

articles.title: The Download: introducing the 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now
articles.summary: MIT Technology Review introduces a guide identifying 10 key AI matters, cutting through hype and constant launches to highlight what genuinely impacts artificial intelligence development and society.

articles.title: AI needs a strong data fabric to deliver business value
articles.summary: AI deployment in enterprises is accelerating across business functions, but organizations face a critical challenge: data quality and context are more important than model performance for delivering business value.

articles.title: 3 things Michelle Kim is into right now
articles.summary: Michelle Kim is interested in Isegye Idol, a Korean virtual idol group where anonymous humans perform as anime-style digital characters via motion capture, playing games and creating humorous content.

articles.title: Gemma 4 VLA Demo on Jetson Orin Nano Super
articles.summary: Gemma 4 VLA (Vision Language Model) demonstrated running on Jetson Orin Nano Super, showcasing efficient AI inference capabilities on edge devices for vision and language tasks.

articles.title: QIMMA قِمّة ⛰: A Quality-First Arabic LLM Leaderboard
articles.summary: QIMMA introduces a quality-focused leaderboard for Arabic Large Language Models, prioritizing evaluation standards over quantity to advance Arabic LLM development.

articles.title: AI and the Future of Cybersecurity: Why Openness Matters
articles.summary: Open approaches to AI cybersecurity enhance threat detection and defense collaboration. Transparency in AI systems enables better security practices and faster vulnerability responses.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development. The conversation explores upcoming trends and innovations in artificial intelligence technology.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing. These AI solutions enable companies to streamline workflows and generate imaginative marketing materials more efficiently.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools publicly available to advance sustainable farming practices and innovation.

articles.title: We're launching two specialized TPUs for the agentic era.
articles.summary: Google launches two specialized TPUs in its eighth generation, designed to power future AI agents and applications.

articles.title: 3 new ways Ads Advisor is making Google Ads safer and faster
articles.summary: Google Ads Advisor introduces three new agentic safety and policy features to protect and streamline Google Ads accounts, enhancing security and efficiency.

articles.title: 7 ways to travel smarter this summer, with help from Google
articles.summary: Google offers seven tools to help travelers plan trips, find deals, and explore destinations this summer, making travel planning easier and more efficient.

articles.title: Partnering with industry leaders to accelerate AI transformation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with global consultancies to help organizations worldwide implement frontier AI technology and accelerate their digital transformation.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS is a new audio model offering granular audio tags for precise control over expressive AI speech generation.

articles.title: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Powering real-world robotics tasks through enhanced embodied reasoning
articles.summary: Gemini Robotics ER 1.6 advances autonomous robotics through improved spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding capabilities, enabling robots to better perform real-world tasks.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency, while Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family. The episode discusses these releases and other major AI developments from the week.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: DLSS 5 uses generative AI to enhance real-time graphics and photorealism in video games. Nvidia's technology applies AI filtering techniques with potential applications beyond gaming.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: Reversing enterprise security costs with AI vulnerability discovery
articles.summary: AI vulnerability discovery is reducing enterprise security costs by identifying exploits faster than traditional methods. Firefox team found 271 vulnerabilities using Claude Mythos Preview, challenging the outdated security doctrine.

articles.title: AI in law firms entering its closing summaries
articles.summary: Law firms are entering a third stage of AI adoption, moving from dismissal to active engagement. Success requires change management, workflow redesign, lawyer retraining, and establishing clear standards for AI use with human oversight.

articles.title: The role of AI in modern forex bot development
articles.summary: AI-powered forex bots are transforming currency trading by automating analysis of vast market data and identifying patterns in real-time. Modern systems use adaptive AI techniques for improved risk evaluation and continuous learning.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agents cause task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing a key challenge in collaborative AI debugging.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for robot navigation that addresses localization, destination identification, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, presenting a significant supply chain security risk.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for faster, concise answers but diminishes their ability to explore alternative solutions on complex problems, revealing a critical performance tradeoff.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering infrastructure around them emerges as the true competitive advantage, as revealed by Anthropic's Claude Code leak.

articles.title: Photon Releases Spectrum: An Open-Source TypeScript Framework that Deploys AI Agents Directly to iMessage, WhatsApp, and Telegram
articles.summary: Photon released Spectrum, an open-source TypeScript framework that deploys AI agents directly to popular messaging platforms like iMessage, WhatsApp, and Telegram, solving the distribution problem by reaching users where they already communicate.

articles.title: OpenAI Open-Sources Euphony: A Browser-Based Visualization Tool for Harmony Chat Data and Codex Session Logs
articles.summary: OpenAI released Euphony, an open-source browser-based visualization tool that transforms structured chat data and Codex session logs into readable, interactive views. It helps developers debug complex AI agents by converting raw JSON into understandable conversation formats.

articles.title: Hugging Face Releases ml-intern: An Open-Source AI Agent that Automates the LLM Post-Training Workflow
articles.summary: Hugging Face released ml-intern, an open-source AI agent that automates LLM post-training workflows by performing literature review, dataset discovery, training, and evaluation tasks autonomously.

articles.title: 5 gifts your mom will actually like for Mother's Day
articles.summary: Mother's Day gift guide featuring useful gadgets and cozy items approved by staff members' moms. Curated selection of practical presents arriving within weeks.

articles.title: This Moto G Stylus phone deal includes a free smartwatch and more - here's how it works
articles.summary: Motorola is offering a promotional deal on the Moto G Stylus phone that includes free accessories: Moto Buds Loop earbuds, Moto Watch, and Moto Tag when purchased directly from Motorola.

articles.title: I set up this Linux 'Watchdog' and now my system auto-reboots when it locks up
articles.summary: A Linux watchdog tool monitors system responsiveness and automatically reboots when the system becomes unresponsive or locks up, preventing prolonged downtime.

articles.title: Train, Serve, and Deploy a Scikit-learn Model with FastAPI
articles.summary: FastAPI is a popular framework for serving machine learning models due to its lightweight, fast, and user-friendly design. It simplifies training, deployment, and serving of Scikit-learn models.

articles.title: AI Agent Memory Explained in 3 Levels of Difficulty
articles.summary: Stateless AI agents lack memory of previous interactions, making each call independent. The article explains agent memory across three difficulty levels.

articles.title: Getting Started with Zero-Shot Text Classification
articles.summary: Zero-shot text classification enables labeling text without requiring task-specific training data, allowing models to classify content based on pre-existing knowledge.

articles.title: From Ad Hoc Prompting to Repeatable AI Workflows with Claude Code Skills
articles.summary: The article demonstrates converting ad hoc LLM prompting into repeatable customer research workflows using Claude's code skills for structured, scalable AI applications.

articles.title: Ivory Tower Notes: The Methodology
articles.summary: The article introduces scientific methodology principles to address poor AI outputs. It emphasizes rigorous, systematic approaches over garbage-in-garbage-out problems in AI systems.

articles.title: How to Run OpenClaw with Open-Source Models
articles.summary: Learn how to run OpenClaw assistant using open-source language models instead of proprietary alternatives, enabling more accessible and customizable AI solutions.

articles.title: From Rainforests to Recycling Plants: 5 Ways NVIDIA AI Is Protecting the Planet
articles.summary: NVIDIA AI technology is being deployed across environmental applications, from rainforest monitoring to recycling plant optimization, helping protect ecosystems and improve sustainability efforts globally.

articles.title: NVIDIA and Google Cloud Collaborate to Advance Agentic and Physical AI
articles.summary: NVIDIA and Google Cloud expanded their decade-long partnership to advance agentic and physical AI, enabling developers to deploy AI agents and robots in production environments. New enhancements to Google Cloud AI Hypercomputer will power the next generation of AI factories.

articles.title: Autonomous AI at Scale: Adobe Agents Unlock Breakthrough Creative Intelligence With NVIDIA and WPP
articles.summary: Adobe, NVIDIA, and WPP collaborate to deploy autonomous AI agents for enterprise marketing, enabling continuous content creation and personalized customer experiences at scale while maintaining brand control.

articles.title: Company-wise memory in Amazon Bedrock with Amazon Neptune and Mem0
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock now enables company-wise memory using Neptune and Mem0, allowing AI chatbots to maintain persistent organizational context for more intelligent, adaptive responses across interactions.

articles.title: From developer desks to the whole organization: Running Claude Cowork in Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Claude Cowork is now available in Amazon Bedrock, enabling organizations to deploy AI across all knowledge workers while maintaining security, data residency, and privacy within AWS environments.

articles.title: End-to-end lineage with DVC and Amazon SageMaker AI MLflow apps
articles.summary: Production ML teams struggle with model lineage traceability across data, code, and experiments. The article demonstrates combining DVC, Amazon SageMaker, and MLflow to enable full audit trails for regulated industries.

articles.title: Maximizing Memory Efficiency to Run Bigger Models on NVIDIA Jetson
articles.summary: Open source generative AI models are being deployed to edge devices like NVIDIA Jetson for robotics and autonomous systems. Memory optimization techniques enable larger models to run on resource-constrained edge hardware.

articles.title: Run High-Throughput Reinforcement Learning Training with End-to-End FP8 Precision
articles.summary: NVIDIA enables high-throughput reinforcement learning training using FP8 precision for LLMs. This approach improves efficiency for reasoning-grade models like GRPO algorithms.

articles.title: Mitigating Indirect AGENTS.md Injection Attacks in Agentic Environments
articles.summary: AI agents accelerate software development by automating coding tasks and serving as real-time copilots. The article addresses security risks of indirect injection attacks in agentic environments.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA explores how generational differences shape consumer attitudes toward ads across various social media platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta committed to achieving net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing these solutions openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background created innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how unconventional career paths can lead to breakthrough innovations in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and gradient reshaping for cleaner action signals.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying feature interactions in Large Language Models at scale to improve model interpretability and transparency. This work aims to make LLM decision-making processes more understandable for safer, more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on designing imaging systems using information-driven principles to optimize how optical systems map objects to measurements. The approach uses noise models to quantify measurement quality and distinguish between objects.
